<doctag><picture><loc_35><loc_34><loc_63><loc_45></picture>
<page_header><loc_407><loc_33><loc_464><loc_42>STUSB4500</page_header>
<page_header><loc_415><loc_42><loc_464><loc_48>Pin description</page_header>
<unordered_list><list_item><loc_83><loc_60><loc_462><loc_80>· At detachment the POWER_OK pins remain enabled (if already asserted), whereas VBUS_EN_SNK is disabled (Hi-Z) to deactivate the V$_{BUS}$ power path from the USB Type-C receptacle. The POWER_OK pins state is reinitialized (Hi-Z) after new attachment or after a reset</list_item>
</unordered_list>
<section_header_level_1><loc_35><loc_89><loc_104><loc_96>2.2.9 GPIO</section_header_level_1>
<text><loc_83><loc_98><loc_410><loc_111>This pin is an active low open drain output that can be configured by NVM as per table below (see Section 5 Start-up configuration).</text>
<otsl><loc_82><loc_130><loc_464><loc_254><fcel>NVM parameter  GPIO_CFG[1:0]<fcel>Pin name<fcel>Pin function<fcel>Value<fcel>Description<nl><fcel>00b<fcel>SW_CTRL_GPIO<fcel>Software controlled GPIO.<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>When bit #0 value is 0b (at start-up)<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>The output state is defined by  the value of 1$^{2}$C register bit #0  at address 2D<fcel>0<fcel>When bit #0 value is 1b<nl><fcel>01b  (default)<fcel>ERROR_RECOVERY<fcel>Hardware fault detection  (see Section 3.8  Hardware fault management)<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No hardware fault detected<nl><ucel><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Hardware fault detected<nl><fcel>10b<fcel>DEBUG<fcel>Debug accessory detection  (see Section 3.8  Debug accessory mode detection)<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No debug accessory detected<nl><ucel><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Debug accessory detected<nl><fcel>11b<fcel>SINK_POWER<fcel>Indicates USB Type-C current  capability advertised by the  source<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>Source supplies default or 1.5 A USB  Type-C current at 5 V<nl><ucel><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Source supplies 3.0 A USB Type-C  current at 5 V<nl><caption><loc_217><loc_121><loc_330><loc_127>Table 5. GPIO pin configuration</caption></otsl>
<section_header_level_1><loc_35><loc_263><loc_145><loc_270>2.2.10 VBUS_EN_SNK</section_header_level_1>
<text><loc_83><loc_272><loc_450><loc_292>This pin allows the incoming V$_{BUS}$ power from the USB Type-C receptacle to be enabled when a source is connected according to different operating conditions stated in the table below. The default behavior of the pin can be changed by NVM programming (see Section 5 Start-up configuration).</text>
<otsl><loc_82><loc_313><loc_464><loc_399><fcel>NVM parameter  POWER_ONLY<fcel>Pin function<fcel>Value<fcel>Description<fcel>Comment<nl><fcel>_ABOVE_5V<fcel>Enable V$_{BUS}$ power path  when source attached  whatever V$_{BUS}$ voltage (5 V or  any PDO voltage)<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No source attached<ucel><nl><fcel>0b  (default)<fcel>Enabled V$_{BUS}$ voltage (5 V or  any PDO voltage)<fcel>0<fcel>Source attached<ucel><nl><fcel>1b<fcel>Enable V$_{BUS}$ power path only  when source attached and  V$_{BUS}$ voltage negotiated to  PDO2 or PDO3 voltage<fcel>Hi-Z<fcel>No source attached or no PD explicit  contract with PDO2 or PDO3<fcel>Valid for all  POWER_OK pin  configurations 1, 2  and 3<nl><ucel><ucel><fcel>0<fcel>Source attached and PD explicit  contract with PDO2 or PDO3<ucel><nl><caption><loc_199><loc_302><loc_349><loc_308>Table 6. VBUS_EN_SNK pin configuration</caption></otsl>
<text><loc_83><loc_407><loc_457><loc_420>When POWER_ONLY_ABOVE_5V bit is set to logic level high, the VBUS_EN_SNK pin is asserted only when a PDO2 or PDO3 explicit contract is established with the source (see Section 3.3 Auto-run mode).</text>
<text><loc_83><loc_422><loc_459><loc_435>This feature is suited for sink devices requiring high power charging profile above 5 V to be fully operational (see Section 6.
